summary: Growing up in Italy, specifically in Tuscany, I was surrounded by beautiful landscapes and historic art that deeply inspired my early interest in art. My family background there also influenced my writing, as I often draw from those experiences. Italy shaped my artistic vision before I moved to the United States to pursue new opportunities.

I moved to the United States from Tuscany, settling in the American West, which was a big change but opened many doors for me in art and printing. Over the years, I have developed my craft here, combining my Italian heritage with new influences. The U.S. has been the place where I built my career and connected with organizations like The Grabhorn Press.

Working with The Grabhorn Press was a wonderful chapter in my life. It allowed me to merge my illustration skills with printing to create beautiful, artistic books. This collaboration reflected my vision and helped me produce some of my most memorable projects, including intricate hand-colored details.

My style of illustration is heavily influenced by the early Renaissance, especially in the use of hand-colored and illuminated details. I enjoy creating unique initials and decorative elements that bring texts to life, inspired by the artistic traditions of that period. This influence has been a constant throughout my career.

entity: The Voyage and Travaile of Sir John Maundevile
summary: One of my favorite projects was illustrating The Voyage and Travaile of Sir John Maundevile. I created thirty-one illustrations and thirty-four hand-illuminated initials for it, each done freestyle with great care. It was truly a labor of love that showcased my dedication to detailed, hand-crafted work.

San Francisco has always felt like a second home to me. Even when I lived elsewhere, my books kept me connected to the city. Now that I am back, I deeply cherish being part of its vibrant cultural life and community.
